- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.text: I'll analyze the test suite and CI configuration to verify that no tests would hard-fail in the lean CI environment. Let me start by examining the CI workflow and the test dependencies.

1	name: Tests
2	
3	on:
4	  push:
5	    branches: [main]
6	  pull_request:
7	    branches: [main]
8	
9	jobs:
10	  test:
11	    name: Phase 0 test suite
12	    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
13	
14	    # Provision Postgres so the checkpointer durability/cross-boundary recovery
15	    # tests run unconditionally in CI instead of silently skipping — that invariant
16	    # is exactly where regressions slip in (#260). The tests fail (not skip) in CI
17	    # if this service is unreachable; see test_postgres_checkpointer.py.
18	    # A SQLite-saver proxy (langgraph-checkpoint-sqlite, the issue's original
19	    # proposal) was viable but rejected: exercising the REAL pooled PostgresSaver
20	    # path here — self-healing pool, advisory-locked migration — covers the actual
21	    # production durability contract, not a different store's semantics.
22	    services:
23	      postgres:
24	        image: postgres:16
25	        env:
26	          POSTGRES_USER: postgres
27	          POSTGRES_PASSWORD: postgres
28	          POSTGRES_DB: hcgrc
29	        ports:
30	          - 5432:5432
31	        options: >-
32	          --health-cmd pg_isready
33	          --health-interval 10s
34	          --health-timeout 5s
35	          --health-retries 5
36	
37	    env:
38	      HCGRC_POSTGRES_URL: postgresql://[REDACTED]@localhost:5432/hcgrc
39	
40	    steps:
41	      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
42	
43	      - name: Set up Python 3.11
44	        uses: actions/setup-python@v5
45	        with:
46	          python-version: "3.11"
47	          cache: "pip"
48	          cache-dependency-path: requirements-ci.txt
49	
50	      - name: Install CI dependencies
51	        run: pip install -r requirements-ci.txt
52	
53	      - name: Run the test suite
54	        # Run ALL tests, not just tests/test_phase0/ — the reasoning_client,
55	        # observability, and infrastructure suites were never gated by CI (pass-7).
56	        # Backend-dependent tests (claude-agent-sdk, phoenix.otel, mlflow) are
57	        # importorskip-guarded so they no-op in the lean CI env; Postgres is provided
58	        # by the service container above.
59	        run: python -m pytest tests/ -v --tb=short
60	
61	      - name: Check for ruff lint errors
62	        run: |
63	          pip install ruff==0.4.8 --quiet
64	          ruff check src/ tests/
65

1	# Minimal dependencies for CI test runs (Phase 0 tests only).
2	# Does NOT include the full ML stack (PyTorch, transformers, etc.) — those are
3	# Phase 1 execution dependencies. CI runs the structural/protocol tests.
4	#
5	# Versions are bounded to the same major as requirements.txt so CI exercises the
6	# versions production runs on (pass-2 #8/#191). The Tier-2/Tier-3 LLM clients
7	# (langchain-ollama, claude-agent-sdk) are intentionally omitted: the suite runs
8	# with HCGRC_DISABLE_REASONING=1 and imports them lazily, so they are never needed.
9	langgraph>=1.2,<2
10	langgraph-checkpoint-postgres>=3.1,<4
11	# Pin the checkpoint core to the exact floor the postgres saver needs
12	# (langgraph-checkpoint-postgres 3.1 requires >=4.1,<5) so CI and production resolve
13	# the same version — parity (#260, pass-2 #191; floor corrected pass-6).
14	langgraph-checkpoint>=4.1,<5
15	# psycopg (pool + binary) backs the pooled PostgresSaver now that the checkpointer
16	# durability tests run against a Postgres service container in CI (#260). Floor
17	# matches the production requirements.txt (>=3.2) so CI exercises the same minor
18	# the node runs, not an older 3.1 (pass-5).
19	psycopg[binary,pool]>=3.2,<4
20	langchain-core>=1.4,<2
21	# reasoning_client imports the OTel API at module load; config loads YAML.
22	opentelemetry-sdk>=1.25,<2
23	PyYAML>=6.0,<7
24	# Match the production floors (requirements.txt) so CI exercises the same major
25	# versions that run on the py3.14 node, not stale ones (#264).
26	numpy>=2.1,<3
27	scikit-learn>=1.5,<2
28	# Major-version ceilings so CI runs the same major as production (requirements.txt
29	# pins pytest==8.2.2 / pytest-cov==5.0.0); without these, pip resolves pytest to
30	# 9.x and CI no longer exercises the version the node runs (#264 pattern, pass-5).
31	pytest>=8.2.2,<9

1	"""
2	bench_checkpointer.py — PostgresSaver throughput benchmark.
3	
4	Phase 0 deliverable #4: measure checkpoint write/read throughput on target hardware.
5	Uses the graph's public API (.invoke, .get_state) — NOT the internal checkpointer.put()
6	API, which is unstable across LangGraph minor versions.
7	
8	Results are logged to MLflow (local tracking, no SaaS).
9	
10	Run with:
11	    python -m pytest tests/benchmarks/bench_checkpointer.py -v -s
12	    -- or --
13	    python tests/benchmarks/bench_checkpointer.py
14	
15	Prerequisites (PostgresSaver path):
16	    - PostgreSQL running on localhost:5432/hcgrc
17	    - HCGRC_POSTGRES_URL env var (optional, defaults to localhost)
18	
19	If Postgres is not available, the PostgresSaver benchmark is skipped (pytest) or
20	reports "unavailable" (standalone runner); the MemorySaver baseline always runs.
21	MemorySaver results are not representative of production throughput.
22	"""
23	
24	from __future__ import annotations
25	
26	import math
27	import time
28	import uuid
29	import warnings
30	from typing import Any
31	
32	import pytest
33	
34	from src.checkpointer import get_checkpointer
35	from src.graph import build_graph
36	from src.state import initial_state
37	
38	
39	def _percentile(sorted_vals: list[float], pct: float) -> float:
40	    """Nearest-rank percentile. Fixes the off-by-one that made p95 == max (#211)."""
41	    if not sorted_vals:
42	        return 0.0
43	    rank = max(1, math.ceil(pct / 100.0 * len(sorted_vals)))
44	    return sorted_vals[min(rank, len(sorted_vals)) - 1]
45	
46	
47	# ── Benchmark parameters ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
48	
49	N_RUNS = 20            # Number of graph runs (each = one write + one read cycle)
50	WRITE_THRESHOLD_MS = 150  # Each checkpoint write < 150ms on target hardware
51	READ_THRESHOLD_MS = 75    # Each state read < 75ms on target hardware
52	
53	
54	# ── Benchmark core ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
55	
56	def run_benchmark(use_memory: bool = False) -> dict[str, Any]:
57	    """
58	    Execute the checkpointer benchmark via the graph's public API.
59	
60	    Each iteration:
61	      1. Invokes the graph up to the Gate 1 interrupt (triggers a checkpoint write)
62	      2. Reads the state snapshot back (exercises the checkpoint read path)
63	
64	    Timing is wall-clock around each step.
65	    """
66	    # Measure checkpoint write/read, NOT LLM inference: the orchestrator node would
67	    # otherwise call the reasoning_client and fold model latency into the timing
68	    # (#234). Disable reasoning + tracing for the duration of the benchmark.
69	    import os
70	    os.environ["HCGRC_DISABLE_REASONING"] = "1"
71	    os.environ["HCGRC_DISABLE_TRACING"] = "1"
72	
73	    checkpointer = get_checkpointer(use_memory=use_memory)
74	    backend = "MemorySaver" if use_memory else "PostgresSaver"
75	    graph = build_graph(checkpointer=checkpointer)
76	
77	    write_times_ms: list[float] = []
78	    read_times_ms: list[float] = []
79	
80	    for _ in range(N_RUNS):
81	        run_id = str(uuid.uuid4())
82	        state = initial_state(run_id=run_id)
83	        config = {"configurable": {"thread_id": run_id}}
84	
85	        # Write: invoke graph until Gate 1 interrupt — checkpointer writes state
86	        t0 = time.perf_counter()
87	        graph.invoke(state, config=config)
88	        write_times_ms.append((time.perf_counter() - t0) * 1000)
89	
90	        # Read: get_state reads the most recent checkpoint
91	        t0 = time.perf_counter()
92	        snapshot = graph.get_state(config)
93	        read_times_ms.append((time.perf_counter() - t0) * 1000)
94	
95	        assert snapshot is not None, "Checkpoint read returned None"
96	        assert snapshot.values.get("run_id") == run_id, "run_id mismatch after checkpoint round-trip"
97	
98	    write_sorted = sorted(write_times_ms)
99	    read_sorted = sorted(read_times_ms)
100	
101	    return {
102	        "backend": backend,
103	        "n_runs": N_RUNS,
104	        "write_p50_ms": _percentile(write_sorted, 50),
105	        "write_p95_ms": _percentile(write_sorted, 95),
106	        "write_max_ms": max(write_times_ms),
107	        "read_p50_ms": _percentile(read_sorted, 50),
108	        "read_p95_ms": _percentile(read_sorted, 95),
109	        "read_max_ms": max(read_times_ms),
110	        "write_threshold_ms": WRITE_THRESHOLD_MS,
111	        "read_threshold_ms": READ_THRESHOLD_MS,
112	        "write_threshold_passed": all(t < WRITE_THRESHOLD_MS for t in write_times_ms),
113	        "read_threshold_passed": all(t < READ_THRESHOLD_MS for t in read_times_ms),
114	    }
115	
116	
117	# ── MLflow logging ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
118	
119	def log_to_mlflow(results: dict[str, Any]) -> None:
120	    """Log benchmark results to MLflow (local tracking server, no SaaS)."""
121	    try:
122	        import mlflow
123	        with mlflow.start_run(run_name=f"checkpointer_benchmark_{results['backend']}"):
124	            mlflow.log_params({
125	                "backend": results["backend"],
126	                "n_runs": results["n_runs"],
127	                "write_threshold_ms": results["write_threshold_ms"],
128	                "read_threshold_ms": results["read_threshold_ms"],
129	            })
130	            mlflow.log_metrics({
131	                "write_p50_ms": results["write_p50_ms"],
132	                "write_p95_ms": results["write_p95_ms"],
133	                "write_max_ms": results["write_max_ms"],
134	                "read_p50_ms": results["read_p50_ms"],
135	                "read_p95_ms": results["read_p95_ms"],
136	                "read_max_ms": results["read_max_ms"],
137	            })
138	    except Exception as e:
139	        warnings.warn(f"MLflow logging failed (non-critical): {e}")
140	
141	
142	# ── pytest integration ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
143
